title: Coix lacryma-jobi var. ma-yuen
what_description: Coix lacryma-jobi var. ma-yuen is a variety of Coix lacryma-jobi, a species of grass belonging to the family Poaceae. It is an annual or perennial herb with a height of up to 2.5 meters, featuring a robust stem and large, ovate leaves that are 10-30 cm long and 5-15 cm wide. The leaves are dark green, glaucous and have a wavy margin. The plant produces spathes that enclose a cluster of flowers, which are arranged in a terminal inflorescence. The flowers are yellowish-brown and 2-3 cm long, with a distinctive, tear-shaped (lacrimal) seed called a caryopsis that is used in traditional medicine.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Coix lacryma-jobi var. ma-yuen are diabetes, which has been treated with a decoction of the plant, as well as gastritis, whose symptoms are alleviated by its anthraquinone content. Hypertension is also managed through the reduction of blood pressure caused by the plant's flavonoids and saponins, while inflammation is alleviated by its anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, asthma, eczema, and skin infections can be treated with the plant's immunomodulatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's antioxidant properties also help alleviate hyperlipidemia, atherosclerosis, and rheumatoid arthritis, and its anti-inflammatory properties help with rheumatism and digestive issues. Furthermore, Coix lacryma-jobi var. ma-yuen has been used to treat anemia, gout, kidney stones, osteoporosis, and psoriasis, as well as high blood pressure and cancer.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Coix lacryma-jobi var. ma-yuen are antioxidant, which helps protect cells from damage and oxidative stress, and anti-inflammatory, reducing swelling and pain in the body. This plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, are responsible for its cardioprotective effects, which help regulate blood pressure and prevent cardiovascular disease. The antimicrobial properties of Coix lacryma-jobi var. ma-yuen, attributed to its volatile oils and phenolic acids, enable it to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms and prevent infections. Additionally, the hepatoprotective properties of this plant, mediated by its flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the liver from damage and promote its regeneration. The plant's unique combination of bioactive compounds also contributes to its medicinal properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Coix lacryma-jobi var. ma-yuen are coixol, phytic acid, saponins, coixene, and triterpenoids. Coixol, a bioactive compound, has been shown to possess hepatoprotective and antioxidant properties. Phytic acid, a polyphenol, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, while saponins are known for their potential in lowering cholesterol levels and exhibiting anti-cancer properties. Coixene, a lignan, has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, while triterpenoids are reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities. These constituents contribute to the plant's traditional use in treating various ailments, including liver disease, fever, and inflammation, and have been scientifically validated through several studies.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Coix lacryma-jobi var. ma-yuen are job's tears tea, which involves steeping dried seeds in hot water to extract their bioactive compounds, job's tears tincture, a concentrated liquid extract made by soaking the seeds in a solvent such as ethanol, job's tears infusion, a liquid extract made by steeping the seeds in cold water, job's tears oil, obtained through the process of cold-pressing or solvent extraction, and job's tears decoction, a liquid extract made by boiling the seeds in water to release their active compounds, these preparations are used for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Coix lacryma-jobi var. ma-yuen improperly include experiencing nausea, cause diarrhea, induce nausea, induce vomiting, and experience abdominal pain. These side effects are primarily due to the high saponin content in the plant, which can cause gastrointestinal irritation. Saponins can also lead to the release of histamine, a chemical that can cause the stomach to contract and lead to vomiting and diarrhea. The improper use of Coix lacryma-jobi var. ma-yuen can also lead to dehydration due to excessive vomiting and diarrhea, which can be life-threatening if left untreated. The severity of these side effects can be exacerbated by the individual's sensitivity to saponins and the dosage of the plant.
